    "decision_notes": "The question asks for a binary conclusion (Yes/No) regarding whether modular flow generates correct time evolution for excited states, along with the scaling law and interpretation. Based on the principles of Tomita-Takesaki theory and the Thermal Time Hypothesis (TTH), modular flow corresponds to the Hamiltonian evolution only for the thermal equilibrium state (KMS state). For excited states, the modular flow deviates from the physical time evolution. The scaling law typically involves the deviation growing with the excitation energy or the system size, and the interpretation relates to the preparation history/state dependence.",
